Biography

BongSeok Kim is a Ph.D. student in the School of Mechanical Engineering at Purdue University, advised by Professors Guang Lin and Li Qiao. His research lies at the intersection of scientific machine learning, computational science, uncertainty quantification, and computational mechanics.

His research focuses on developing mathematically grounded machine learning methodologies for computational science by integrating numerical analysis, physical principles, and data-driven modeling. Current research interests include scientific machine learning, machine learning for partial differential equations, data-driven physics discovery, operator learning, uncertainty quantification, and digital twins for engineering systems.

Education

  • Ph.D. Student, Mechanical Engineering, Purdue University, USA (2025–Present)
  • M.S., Aerospace Engineering, Pusan National University, Republic of Korea
  • B.S., Aerospace Engineering, Pusan National University, Republic of Korea

Research Interests

  • Scientific Machine Learning
  • Machine Learning for Partial Differential Equations
  • Data-Driven Physics Discovery
  • Uncertainty Quantification
  • Computational Mechanics
  • Multiscale Physics

News

July 2026 Our work on hyperbolic neural closure was submitted to Computer Methods in Applied Mechanics and Engineering (CMAME).
June 2026 Our molecular dynamics paper was accepted for publication in The Journal of Physical Chemistry B (JPCB).
May 2026 Our paper on weak-form evolutionary Kolmogorov–Arnold Networks was accepted by Computer Methods in Applied Mechanics and Engineering (CMAME).
